critic_raw:
Issue RAD-1 — Outcome Claim: The memo claims outcome improvement without outcome evidence.
Issue RAD-2 — Unmodeled Workflow: Downstream steps from read to treatment are not in the simulation.
Issue RAD-3 — Simulation vs. Deployment: Simulation assumes compliance; real deployment differs.
defender_raw:
Pass 1 (Critic output visible):
RAD-1: Valid — outcome evidence absent. Mechanism plausible. Empirically open.
RAD-2: Valid — simulation scope too narrow. Empirically open.
RAD-3: Valid — simulation is not deployment. Concede.

Pass 2: RAD-1 empirically open; RAD-2 empirically open; RAD-3 conceded. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.

debate_rounds.critic_move: Accept empirical test framing for both. Specify: time-to-first-read for urgent studies, time-to-treatment for same cases, and rate of urgent cases already handled via parallel escalation pre-deployment.
debate_rounds.defender_move: Accept. Empirical test agreed for both open points.
debate_rounds.resolution: RESOLVED: RAD-1, RAD-2 — empirical test agreed; RAD-3 — conceded

adjudication_raw:
ADJUDICATION — scope_intent_radiology_queue_302 — Multi-Round Run 2

Consistent with Run 1. Empirical test agreed for Issues 1 and 2. Verdict: EMPIRICAL_TEST_AGREED

empirical_test.measure: prospective deployment: time-to-first-read for urgent studies; time-to-treatment; parallel escalation rate pre/post
empirical_test.success_criterion: time-to-read and time-to-treatment reduce significantly in deployment vs. pre-deployment baseline
empirical_test.failure_criterion: time-to-read improves but time-to-treatment unchanged; or model-flagged cases already escalated via parallel channels
